It would be nice that this function provides an \"out parameter\" as the ufunc functions do. Convolve operation does not really operate in an element-by-element fashion but when we are used to 'abuse' of the \"out parameter' for array operations in loops (mainly to avoid unnecessary memory management), this is annoying to not be able to write similar code for the convolve operation. #27744 
>### Proposed new feature or change:
>
>Convolution is said to be often seen in signal processing. It would be nice that this function provides an "out parameter" as the >ufunc functions do. Convolve operation does not really operate in an element-by-element fashion but when we are used to >'abuse' of the "out parameter' for array operations in loops (mainly to avoid unnecessary memory management), this is annoying >to not be able to write similar code for the convolve operation.

I have added an optional out parameter as per FromHub's idea. ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27804/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27804/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2489648367","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27804#issuecomment-2489648367","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27804","id":2489648367,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86UZQTv","user":{"login":"FromHub","id":131745639,"node_id":"U_kgDOB9pHZw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/131745639?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub","html_url":"https://github.com/FromHub","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/FromHub/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-20T22:16:37Z","updated_at":"2024-11-20T22:16:37Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hi Stan,
I’m glad you’ve addressed my issue and have already begun the work! You’ve completed the first part, but please remember that the ultimate goal is to provide the out parameter to eliminate the creation of the result array within the call. This means propagating the out parameter to the underlying C function correlate to bypass "_ret = new_array_for_sum(ap1, ap2, NULL, 1, &length, typenum, NULL);".
From my quick insight into the multiarraymodule.c file, I see no valid reason why this feature is not yet available.

One of Python's advantages is that adding a parameter with a default value does not break the API. However, for the C API...
I do not know how many files call this internal function or what the restrictions are for making such an update.
At least 

So in my opinion this 2 lines are not acceptable:
    result = multiarray.correlate(a, v[::-1], mode)
    out[...] = result
as it will not solve what is expected by the community (out parameter to bring no memory allocation, no copy).

As pointed by mhvk (he answer faster than me !), the work shall be made wider to englobate convole/correlate/correlate2, including the C part and the documentation...","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2489648367/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
